page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate single-core capability, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average tier. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower-tier multi-threaded decompression and multi-core Geekbench results. Memory bandwidth tests also show weaker performance for both small and large block read/write operations. Despite these constraints, the instance maintains average throughput for static web serving and small-scale LLM inference tasks.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.g7se.xlarge offers a standard resource density suitable for entry-level enterprise workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications such as lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments that do not require high-speed local storage or intensive multi-threaded compute capabilities.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-based instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth remain in the lower tiers. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without extreme I/O demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments.
